info.title: AP Service
info.description: Accounts payable: bills, purchase orders, payment runs, vendor management, and AP aging.

get.description:
Returns AP aging bucket totals grouped by currency as of `as_of`.
Optionally includes a per-vendor breakdown when `by_vendor=true`.

Only bills with status `approved` or `partially_paid` and a positive
remaining open balance are included. Paid and voided bills are excluded.

parameters.name: as_of
parameters.description: Aging reference date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today (UTC).

parameters.name: by_vendor
parameters.description: When `true`, include per-vendor breakdown in the response.

get.description: List all allocations for a bill in insertion order.

post.description:
Apply a payment allocation to an approved or partially-paid bill.
Idempotent: duplicate allocation_id returns the existing record (200 OK).

get.description: Return remaining open balance for a bill.

post.description:
Create a payment run by selecting all eligible bills for the tenant.
Idempotent: supplying the same `run_id` returns the existing run (200 OK).

get.description: Fetch a payment run and its items.

post.description:
Execute a payment run: submit payments to the disbursement layer,
record allocations, mark bills paid, and emit `ap.payment_executed` events.

Idempotent: calling this endpoint on an already-completed run returns the
existing execution state with 200 OK.

Transitions the PO from draft → approved and emits ap.po_approved.
If the PO is already approved, returns 200 with the current state (no re-emit).

ApTaxSnapshot.description:
Persisted tax snapshot for an AP vendor bill.

Records the full quote -> commit -> void lifecycle with provider references
and a deterministic quote hash for idempotency.

ApTaxSummaryRow.description: A single row in an AP paid-tax summary report.

ApiError.description:
Standard API error envelope.

`error` is a machine-readable code (`not_found`, `validation_error`, etc.).
`message` is human-readable.  `request_id` is populated from the tracing
context already present in request extensions.  `details` carries per-field
errors for 422 responses.

ApproveBillRequest.description:
Request body to approve a vendor bill.

Match policy is enforced unless `override_reason` is provided:
  - Bills in 'open' status (never matched) require an override.
  - Bills in 'matched' status with tolerance violations require an override.

approved_by.description: Actor approving the bill (for audit attribution)

override_reason.description:
Override reason to bypass match policy. Required when the bill is
unmatched or has tolerance violations.

ApprovePoRequest.description: Request body to approve a purchase order.

approved_by.description: Actor performing the approval (for event attribution and audit)

AssignTermsRequest.description: Request to assign payment terms to an existing bill.

BillLineRecord.description: A single line on a vendor bill as returned from the DB.

quantity.description: Quantity (DOUBLE PRECISION in DB, f64 in Rust)

CreateAllocationRequest.description: Request body to apply a payment allocation to a vendor bill.

allocation_id.description:
Caller-generated stable UUID — used for idempotency.
Duplicate submissions with the same allocation_id return the existing record.

amount_minor.description: Amount to apply in minor currency units (must be > 0).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Must match the bill currency.

payment_run_id.description: Optional: payment run claiming this allocation (may be set later).

CreateBillLineRequest.description: A single bill line in the creation request.

description.description: Optional description (defaults to "" if not supplied)

gl_account_code.description: GL account code (defaults to "" if not supplied)

item_id.description: Optional item reference — informational only, not persisted in this bead

po_line_id.description: PO line reference for matching (None for unmatched/non-PO bills)

CreateBillRequest.description: Request body to create a vendor bill.

due_date.description: Explicit due date; if None, derived deterministically from vendor payment terms

fx_rate_id.description:
GL fx_rates UUID — supply when bill currency != tenant functional currency.
Reuses GL FX infrastructure; do not pass a raw rate value here.

tax_minor.description: Optional tax total in minor currency units

vendor_invoice_ref.description: Vendor's external invoice reference (must be unique per vendor per tenant)

CreatePaymentTermsRequest.description: Request body to create payment terms.

discount_days.description: Days within which discount applies. Defaults to 0.

installment_schedule.description: Installment schedule as a JSON array (optional).

CreatePoLineRequest.description: A single line in a PO create or update request.

description.description: Human-readable line description. Required when item_id is absent.

item_id.description:
Optional inventory item UUID reference.
Validated as non-empty if provided; no cross-DB reads are performed.
When description is absent, stored as "item:{uuid}".

unit_price_minor.description: Unit price in minor currency units (must be >= 0)

CreatePoRequest.description: Request body to create a purchase order (always created as draft).

created_by.description: Actor creating the PO (for event attribution and audit)

CreateVendorRequest.description: Request body to create a new vendor.

party_id.description: Optional link to a Party record in the party-master service.

payment_terms_days.description: Payment terms in days. Use PaymentTermsPreset for named values.

matched_amount_minor.description: Amount matched at PO price (minor currency units)

po_line_id.description: PO line matched against (None for non-PO lines)

qty_variance.description: bill_qty - (received_qty or po_qty); positive means over-billed

receipt_id.description: Primary receipt used (None for two_way or non_po)

fully_matched.description: True when all lines are within tolerance

PaginatedResponse_PaymentTerms.description:
Generic paginated response envelope.

Every list endpoint returns this wrapper so consumers get consistent
pagination metadata regardless of the underlying entity type.

items.description: Payment terms record as stored in the database.

PaginatedResponse_PurchaseOrder.description:
Generic paginated response envelope.

Every list endpoint returns this wrapper so consumers get consistent
pagination metadata regardless of the underlying entity type.

items.description: Purchase order header record as returned from the DB.

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft, approved, closed, cancelled

total_minor.description: Total PO value in minor currency units

PaginatedResponse_Vendor.description:
Generic paginated response envelope.

Every list endpoint returns this wrapper so consumers get consistent
pagination metadata regardless of the underlying entity type.

items.description: Full vendor record as stored and returned.

party_id.description: Optional link to a Party record in the party-master service.

payment_terms_days.description: Net payment terms in calendar days (e.g. 30 = Net-30)

remittance_email.description: Remittance email address (pointer, not a credential)

PaginatedResponse_VendorBill.description:
Generic paginated response envelope.

Every list endpoint returns this wrapper so consumers get consistent
pagination metadata regardless of the underlying entity type.

items.description: A vendor bill header record as returned from the DB.

fx_rate_id.description: GL fx_rates UUID — NULL when bill currency == functional currency (no FX needed)

tax_minor.description: Tax amount in minor currency units (optional)

total_minor.description: Total bill amount in minor currency units

PaymentTerms.description: Payment terms record as stored in the database.

PoLineRecord.description: A single PO line as returned from the DB.

PurchaseOrder.description: Purchase order header record as returned from the DB.

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft, approved, closed, cancelled

total_minor.description: Total PO value in minor currency units

PurchaseOrderWithLines.description: PO with its line items (for GET and POST 201 responses).

RunMatchRequest.description: Request to run the match engine for a vendor bill against a PO.

matched_by.description: Actor triggering the match (for audit trail)

po_id.description: PO to match this bill against

price_tolerance_pct.description:
Price tolerance as a fraction (0.0–1.0). Default: 0.05 (5%).
Variance ≤ po_price × matched_qty × tolerance is within tolerance.

UpdatePaymentTermsRequest.description: Request body to update payment terms (partial update).

UpdatePoLinesRequest.description: Request body to replace all lines on a draft PO (idempotent full replacement).

lines.description: Full replacement line set (at least one required)

UpdateVendorRequest.description: Request body to update an existing vendor. All fields are optional (partial update).

party_id.description: Optional link to a Party record in the party-master service.

updated_by.description: Actor performing the update (for event attribution)

Vendor.description: Full vendor record as stored and returned.

party_id.description: Optional link to a Party record in the party-master service.

payment_terms_days.description: Net payment terms in calendar days (e.g. 30 = Net-30)

remittance_email.description: Remittance email address (pointer, not a credential)

VendorBill.description: A vendor bill header record as returned from the DB.

fx_rate_id.description: GL fx_rates UUID — NULL when bill currency == functional currency (no FX needed)

tax_minor.description: Tax amount in minor currency units (optional)

total_minor.description: Total bill amount in minor currency units

VendorBillWithLines.description: A vendor bill with its line items (for GET responses and POST 201).

VoidBillRequest.description:
Request body to void a vendor bill.

Voiding is append-only: the void_reason is persisted in the outbox event
and serves as the immutable audit record. Void is allowed from any active
status (open, matched, approved, partially_paid).

void_reason.description: Required reason for voiding (forms the auditable justification)
